review_comment: Are these meaningful checks? Can absl::make_unique ever return nullptr? Is there some other  check that would make this more useful of a test?
Yeah, this is basically to make sure that they're not abstract classes. (meaning all methods are mocked) I guess this should be enough.
Would a more useful test be then to actually derive mocks from these and then use them the way that a user actually would? I'm not fond of the idea of having a test that's basically just a build.

I don't think it gives more test coverage, though because it's testing the mock feature of gtest. If the class is ready to be mockable, I think it's enough for this test? This test is designed to make sure those classes are mockable. (Previously those were not)
